title: Vision Scientists of the Past — Historical Resource
name: Vision Scientists of the Past
description: A near-comprehensive list of deceased vision scientists compiled by Lothar Spillmann and colleagues, with brief biographical notes. This resource is intended as a living document for the vision science community.
website: Vision Scientists of the  Past, Version February 10, 2026d.pdf

title: Manfred MacKeben (1940–2026)
name: Manfred MacKeben
affiliation: Smith-Kettlewell Eye Research Institute, San Francisco, California, USA
description: A prominent vision scientist of five decades standing at the Smith-Kettlewell Eye Research Institute in San Francisco. His work included cortical neurophysiology, transient attention, peripheral vision, low vision rehabilitation, macular vision loss, reading assessment, and diagnostic tools for visual impairment. He received the Constance Atwell Award from ARVO and the Lifetime Achievement Award in Low Vision Research from Envision University.

title: Tim Meese (1966–2026)
name: Tim Meese
description: A leading vision scientist and Professor of Vision Science at Aston University whose research helped shape modern understanding of contrast perception, binocular vision, visual masking, gain control, and spatial vision. He was widely respected as a mentor, collaborator, and advocate for open and reproducible vision research.

title: Robert (Bob) M. Steinman (1927–2026)
name: Robert M. Steinman
affiliation: Department of Psychology, University of Maryland, College Park
description: A pioneering vision scientist whose work transformed understanding of eye movements, fixation, and active vision. His research on microsaccades, smooth eye movements, fixation stability, anticipatory pursuit, and natural gaze behavior advanced theories of active vision and influenced generations of researchers in eye movement science and visual neuroscience.

title: Ariane Lévy-Schoen (1927–2026)
name: Ariane Lévy-Schoen
affiliation: Laboratoire de Psychologie Expérimentale, CNRS, Université René Descartes (EPHE, EHESS), Paris
description: A leading figure in French vision science and Research Director at the CNRS. Her work spanned active visual perception, visuomotor space, eye movement control, saccadic programming, and eye movements in reading. With J. K. O'Regan, she helped revive research on eye movements in reading and co-edited Eye Movements: From Physiology to Cognition.
